tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I've been thinking about our discussion group and want to make sure I have all the relevant information about our members and how they've been interacting. Could you help me review what we already have stored about everyone? I'd love to see the details we've collected about each person's preferences and their participation in the community.
I'll search for the information we have stored about your discussion group members and their interactions.

{"status": "success", "tool": "search_memories", "query": "discussion group members preferences participation community", "limit": 10, "results": [], "message": "No memories found matching the query"}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "list_memories", "memories": [], "user": null}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
It looks like we don't have any information stored yet about your discussion group members or their preferences and participation. The memory system is currently empty.

Would you like to start adding information about your group members? If so, I can help you store details about each person, including their preferences, how they like to participate, and any other relevant information you'd like to track. Just let me know what you'd like to add!

I see - the system is empty right now. That's okay, let me try searching anyway. Could you look for any information about members who prefer written discussions versus those who like to chat verbally, or perhaps anyone who's mentioned having specific communication preferences? I'd like to understand how our group members like to engage even if we haven't stored much yet.
{"status": "success", "results": [], "query": "written discussions verbal chat communication preferences", "count": 0}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
No memories found for communication preferences either. The system truly doesn't have any stored information about your discussion group members yet.

Would you like to:
1. **Add information** about your members and their preferences (like communication style preferences)?
2. **Try a different search query** if you think there might be information stored under different terms?

Let me know how you'd like to proceed!

Yes, I'd love to start adding some information! Let me think... I recently had a nice conversation with Maria during our last group meeting. She's one of our newer members, and I noticed she tends to prefer smaller group discussions rather than speaking up in larger gatherings. She also mentioned she finds it easier to process her thoughts when she can write things down first rather than responding on the spot. Could we store that information about her preferences so I can keep better track of how to support her in future sessions?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Maria - newer member preferences: prefers smaller group discussions rather than speaking up in larger gatherings; finds it easier to process thoughts when she can write things down first rather than responding on the spot. Support tip: offer written preparation time or smaller breakout groups for her to feel more comfortable participating."}
